symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse crampy abdominal pain relieved partially by bowel motions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Crampy visceral pain that eases after motions fits mediator-driven gut smooth-muscle spasm.
Diffuse, non-localising, colicky pain relieved by defaecation argues against localised appendiceal inflammation, though it does not exclude it.
Secretory diarrhoea is a recognised mast-cell mediator effect on the gut.
Diarrhoea is non-specific and also fits gastroenteritis — a non-discriminating feature in isolation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute abdominal bloating with visible distension

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid mediator-driven bowel wall oedema and dysmotility produce visible distension.
Distension can occur in obstruction; coexisting diarrhoea and post-prandial onset argue against.
Flushing is a hallmark mast-cell mediator (histamine/PGD2) effect and strongly points away from a purely surgical or infective cause.
Flushing is not a feature of infective gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic presyncope with ongoing fluid loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mediator-driven vasodilation plus GI fluid loss cause hypotension and near-syncope.
Presyncope mandates active exclusion of evolving anaphylactic circulatory compromise via an airway/breathing screen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid post-prandial onset after a previously tolerated food, with recurrent prior milder flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stereotyped, recurrent, food-triggered multi-system episodes are the diagnostic backbone of a mast-cell mediator disorder.
A 30-minute post-prandial onset to a known food, recurrent pattern, and no sick contacts fit poorly with infective gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patient-reported palpitations / sense of racing heart

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia from hypovolaemia/vasodilation; patient-obtainable as a subjective sensation only, not a measured rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft abdomen, no guarding, no rebound, negative Murphy's and Rovsing's signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of peritonism and negative provocative signs argue strongly against appendicitis/peritonitis — but this is a clinician exam finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain, so it stays sealed and drives safety-netting rather than telehealth exclusion.
A soft, non-tender-on-deep-palpation abdomen with passage of stool argues against complete obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperactive high-pitched bowel sounds with tympanic distension on percussion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperactive sounds fit mediator-driven hypermotility; a sealed clinician finding not obtainable via chat.
Absence of fever lowers the probability of an infective/inflammatory cause.
Afebrile state modestly lowers appendicitis probability but does not exclude early appendicitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No blood in stool or vomit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of GI bleeding lowers concern for ischaemic bowel; its appearance would mandate emergency escalation.
A mediator flare is typically non-bleeding; absence of blood is consistent with the working diagnosis.

investigation_details.expected_result: A transient rise in tryptase during/shortly after the flare compared with baseline supports mast-cell activation; a normal single level does not exclude a GI-predominant flare. Hospital/pathology-collected, not available in real time via telehealth.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Biochemical confirmation of mast-cell activation is missed; diagnosis rests on the clinical pattern and specialist work-up.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A documented transient tryptase rise is a key biochemical criterion for mast-cell activation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: No single feature is diagnostic, but rapid post-prandial cramping diarrhoea + flushing + presyncope + a recurrent food-triggered pattern together form a high-probability mast-cell mediator flare constellation that distinguishes it from an isolated surgical or infective cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports biochemical confirmation of mast-cell activation; best value if a sample is taken close to the flare and compared with a baseline, arranged via GP/immunology — not obtainable in real time in telehealth.
Quantifies dehydration and screens for acute kidney injury given the fluid loss and orthostatic symptoms; guides whether oral rehydration is sufficient. Arranged with the prompt GP review.

Not an appropriate first-line telehealth-driven investigation for a typical flare with a negative history red-flag screen — reserved for red-flag deterioration (peritonism, GI bleeding, pain out of proportion) and ordered by the in-person/ED team, not reflexively to 'exclude the mimic'.
